title: SieveError loses ParseError source chain
description: From<ParseError> for SieveError consumes the original ParseError and bakes it into a String message. SieveError::source() returns None (default impl). Callers using anyhow/thiserror error chain tools will lose the original parse position (line/col). Also, the Lex vs Parse kind classification uses a heuristic (line.is_some()) that will silently misclassify if the form parser ever gains position tracking. The original ParseError should be preserved as a Box<dyn Error> source, or SieveError should carry the structured position data directly.
